Appearance

On this page

Case Study: Market Entry for a US Tech Firm ​

Navigating the FDI regulatory maze to set up a compliant offshore development center.

Industry: Technology / Software
Service: FDI Advisory & Setup
Outcome: 100% Repatriation Compliance

🛑 The Challenge ​

A US-based SaaS company wanted to set up a development center in Kathmandu to tap into local talent. They were concerned about:

  1. FDI Approval Delays: Doing Business in Nepal is notoriously slow.
  2. Repatriation: Fear of getting profits "stuck" in Nepal due to NRB regulations.
  3. IP Protection: Ensuring their code ownership remained in the US.

🛠️ The Artha Approach ​

We acted as their "Local CFO Office," handling everything from the first application to the first payroll.

Phase 1: Structuring (The "Software Export" Model) ​

We registered the company under the Information Technology category.

  • Why?: To avail the 1% Income Tax Rate on export income (as per Finance Act 2079).

Phase 2: The Fast-Track FDI ​

We utilized the automatic route (online submission) for the Department of Industry (DOI).

  • Action: Prepared a robust "Project Report" highlighting the technology transfer aspect, which accelerated the approval to just 12 days.

Phase 3: Banking & Forex ​

We set up a foreign currency account and obtained the "FDI Recording" certificate from Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) immediately upon capital inflow.

  • Crucial Step: Without this certificate, future repatriation is impossible.

💡 The Solution ​

We built a compliance framework that runs on autopilot:

  1. Transfer Pricing Policy: Drafted a "Cost Plus 15%" agreement between the US Parent and Nepal Subsidiary to satisfy tax authorities in both countries.
  2. Monthly Compliance: Outsourced payroll and TDS filing to our team.
  3. Expat Visas: Secured business visas for the visiting US CTO.

🚀 The Impact ​

  • Speed: Company incorporated and operational in 45 days (vs. industry average of 90 days).
  • Tax Efficiency: The company pays an effective tax rate of just 1% on its revenue.
  • Security: Successfully repatriated the first tranche of dividends in 2024 without a single query from the Central Bank.

"We were terrified of Nepalese bureaucracy. The team at Artha made it invisible. We just focus on coding; they handle the government." — Founder, [Client Name Redacted]
